explanation: very simple exterior ambiences. The exterior equivalent of a room tone. If noticable activity find a better fitting category.
for recordings and ambiences geographically linked to Alpine Environments. Some overlap with AMBIENCE-RURAL.
Theme parks and carnivals. Beware of possible rights issues with uncleared music. Carnivals
like a dawn chorus of pretty birds
where the birds are the prominent sound. Single birds would go in BIRDS-SONGBIRD.
New Years Eve. Not pure crowds
as that would go in CROWDS
but i.e. exterior city new years. Also see AMBIENCES-AMUSEMENT for something like a fair or festival.
Construction scenes with things like jackhammers
Quiet desert ambiences with appropriate insects and birds. Maybe tumbleweeds.
Artificial designed ambience. V8.2 adds AMBIENCE-FANTASY and AMBIENCE-SCIFI which may be better fit for most 'designed' categories.
ambulances and medics. Also see VEHICLES-EMERGENCY.
Magical or other wordly composite ambiences
etc. Isolated farm animals would go into ANIMALS-FARM.
Forest recordings or forest composite files. Trees and birds
maybe wind in trees all together. Some overlap with AMBIENCE-RURAL or AMBIENCE-ALPINE.
etc. But FARMS has own category if farm animals or human activity are present.
Roman street scenes. Composite history and ancient ambiences.
control rooms. James Bond type lairs. Spaceship interiors.
surgery and emergency rooms too. Possibly veterinarian hospitals too.
plants or any ambience with an industrial feeling.
cicada and cricket beds mainly. May contain over sounds but main sound being large presence of insects. See also ANIMALS-INSECT for more isolated sounds.
but the lake version of AMBIENCE-SEASIDE. See also WATER-LAPPING.
Busy exterior or interior market activity and crowds. Think vibrant international markets in Istanbul or Africa
Ambience sounds not fitting another category in this list.
things revolving around ships. (compare to AMBIENCE-SEASIDE). Water only recordings would go to a WATER category.
telephones. General offices of various sizes.
probably usually with some human activity
city activity. Not pure nature sounds.
Riots. Big mixed ambiences of crowds
for any other usually indoor public places such as stores
museums. Lots of possible overlap with other categories. Use if not fitting elsewhere.
pilgrimages. Any ambience relating to religion.
Something busier than a room tone
a House with some activity from outside etc. Interior with exterior sounds coming through closed or open doors
mixed crowd with glass clinks or silverware.
Simple room tones with little or no activity. Also see now AMBIENCE-RESIDENTIAL if more than just air is present
as it might be a better fit.
Rural and countryside ambiences that don't fit somewhere else in this list. Nature
a complex background layered with many sci-fi elements. A complex spacehip bridge
or high-tech lab. See also SCIFI for individual elements.
Relating to beach and oceanside scenes with people. (Compare to AMBIENCE-NAUTICAL). Water only recordings would go to a WATER category.
little league baseball to major league. Use CROWDS-SPORT for clean crowd recordings.
A wetland often partially or intermittently covered with water; especially : one dominated by woody vegetation. Marshes
Something in between a CITY and a SUBURB. Activity
that 'small town' or village sound. May be confusion between this
Specifically traffic without people. Not for a single car by
clean highway traffic for example. Rush hour where the sound is predominantly cars.
subway stations. Not the airplane or train itself. But public places centering around transportation.
Treeless environments where the ground is permanently frozen
deep bubbles and that classic undersea world sound. See also WATER-UNDERWATER.
Dense cities and environments. New York City or London or Mumbai. Often Dense Traffic
but really any city. Overlap with AMBIENCE-TOWN possible.
full ambiences here. Not for isolated WEAPONS or VEHICLES which have their own categories. Composite sounds of war.
